response.justification: James is a versatile, seasoned engineer with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD), open-source leadership, and some high-throughput systems exposure at Dunnhumby. However, he falls well short of the core requirement of 8 years of C++ experience — his C++ work is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, with his systems work primarily in DLang and his recent years focused on TypeScript/Node.js product engineering. He also lacks demonstrated cloud compute platform architecture ownership and formal technical-leadership experience mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers, making him a weak fit for this specific role despite his general seniority.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering-practice advocacy: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus mentoring via workshops, dojos, and courses
Long-running ownership of an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes, showing sustained project leadership
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ requirement; systems experience is mostly DLang, recent work is TypeScript/Node.js
No production-level cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure architecture ownership at the required depth
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment for platform teams
